description_long: This object is a pair of pliers with a simple, utilitarian design, featuring two elongated, straight handles that converge at a central pivot. The jaws are V-shaped and appear to be flat, lacking any serration or additional features such as wire cutters. The entire tool is composed of a uniform metallic material, giving it a sleek, industrial appearance. The handles do not show any ergonomic shaping or insulation, suggesting a basic, all-metal construction. The pivot is a single, fixed joint, allowing the jaws to open and close symmetrically. The surface finish is smooth and matte, with a consistent gray coloration throughout.
description: A basic, all-metal pair of pliers with straight handles and flat, V-shaped jaws, finished in matte gray. The design is simple and utilitarian, with no visible grip pattern or insulation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the pliers, with both straight handles and the V-shaped jaws clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the pliers, with the handles and jaws appearing as narrow lines.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the straight handles and V-shaped jaws.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the central pivot point.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, uninsulated metal handles with no ergonomic features.
attribute_jaw_design: Flat, V-shaped jaws with no serration or special features; standard geometry for basic gripping.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Single fixed pivot joint, non-adjustable, allowing symmetrical opening and closing.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal surface with no visible grip pattern or texturing.
